tool.name: skani sketch
tool.description: build a consolidated sketch database from genome assemblies
tool.command: skani sketch -t \${GALAXY_SLOTS:-1} @PRESET_CMD@ -c $sketch_params.c -m $sketch_params.m $individual_seqs -o sk_db #for $g in $genomes '$g' #end for && tar -cf '$sketch_db' -C . sk_db

param.name: genomes
param.label: Genomes to sketch
param.help: One or more FASTA files (gzip accepted).

param.name: individual_seqs
param.label: Treat each sequence as its own genome
param.help: Use individual sequences in multi-fastas as separate genomes (incompatible with --separate-sketches).

param.label: Compression factor (k-mer subsampling rate)
param.help: Lower values give more accurate ANI/AF at the cost of speed and memory. Overridden by the speed/accuracy preset above.

param.label: Marker k-mer compression factor
param.help: Lower values (~200-300) help with small genomes such as plasmids or viruses.

tool.help:
**skani sketch**
Build a consolidated sketch database from a collection of genome assemblies. The sketch database can then be queried efficiently by ``skani search`` -- this is the recommended workflow when you want to compare many query genomes against a fixed reference set, since sketching only needs to happen once.
-----
**Inputs**
- One or more FASTA files (``.fasta`` or ``.fasta.gz``).
-----
**Output**
A ``tar`` archive containing the consolidated sketch database (three files: ``index.db``, ``markers.bin``, ``sketches.db``). Pass this archive directly to the ``skani search`` tool as the *Sketch database* input.
-----
**Tips**
- The sketch parameters (compression factor ``-c`` and marker compression ``-m``) are baked into the database and must match between sketch and query. Pick the preset and parameters at sketch time for the kind of genomes you expect. - For viruses or plasmids (< 20 kb), set the compression factor ``-c`` to 30 and the marker compression ``-m`` to 200 in the *Sketch parameters* section. - For very fragmented MAGs, use the **Slow** preset to capture more aligned fraction.
